world.title: China Tankers Join Line to Test Hormuz Exit and Iran Truce
world.description: Two fully laden Chinese oil tankers are waiting outside the Strait of Hormuz, putting them in a position to become the first such vessels to leave the Persian Gulf under a day-old US-Iran ceasefire, even as shipowners scrutinize the status of the narrow waterway.

world.title: Vietnam eyes up to 50 million visitors by 2030 as tourism boom gets under way
world.description: Vietnam is targeting 45-50 million foreign arrivals annually by 2030 under an updated plan unveiled by the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism, representing average growth of 16-19% a year from now. - VnExpress International

world.title: Trump issues first warning to Iran after ceasefire, wants 'REAL AGREEMENT' to be followed
world.description: A day after announcing a ceasefire, Donald Trump warned Iran that US forces will remain deployed until a “real agreement” is fully enforced. He stressed no nuclear weapons and an open Strait of Hormuz, cautioning that any violation could trigger a stronger military response.

world.title: Africa: Credit and Credibility - Rating Agency Errors Come With a Cost
world.description: Analysis - The rating agency S&P Global's Africa Credit Rating Trends 2025 reviews the past year's rating activities and analyses the continent's prospects for 2026. It is an important document because it interprets underlying drivers of creditworthiness. It shapes how global investors and policymakers understand risk, opportunity and reform dynamics across the continent.

world.title: Saudi Arabia’s Hajj Ministry unveils discount initiative for pilgrims via Nusuk Card
world.description: Middle East News: Saudi Arabia's Ministry of Hajj and Umrah has launched the Nusuk Card, providing discounts and services for Hajj and Umrah pilgrims, enhancing their overall experience and comfort during the pilgrimage.

world.title: Trump: US military to stay around Iran; threatens action if Tehran fails to comply with deal
world.description: US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday its military ships and aircraft will remain around Iran and threatened that the US will start "shooting" again unless Tehran fully complies with the deal reached with Washington.

world.title: New entry/exit system to be implemented for European countries, including Greece
world.description: New EU Entry/Exit System (EES) rolls out across Europe, replacing passport stamping with biometric checks, as airports warn of delays and travel disruptions amid Middle East conflict.

business.title: Wheat Moves Higher on Iran Ceasefire Uncertainty, Crop Survey
business.description: Wheat futures have moved higher in the face of growing signs of fragility in the ceasefire pact between the US and Iran in the Middle East, with any reigniting of tensions likely to threaten further supply chain disruptions and price rises for vital farm inputs.

science.title: Artemis II crew will endure 3,000°C on re-entry. A hypersonics expert explains how they will survive
science.description: The high-speed, hypersonic and extremely hot re-entry is the last challenge the Artemis II crew will have to endure on their epic 10-day mission.

science.title: At 40,000kmh and 2,700 degrees Celsius, Artemis II re-entry will be the scariest phase of the mission yet
science.description: Return of Artemis II on April 10 will be a risky affair because of the heating issues with the spacecraft. It will tumble down at enormous speeds, causing temperatures outside the capsule to rise. Here is how the Orion re-entry will go down.

science.title: Lunar soil 'time capsule' reveals organic evolution in solar system
science.description: Scientists have identified multiple nitrogen-containing organic compounds on the surfaces of lunar soil samples returned by China's Chang'e-5 and Chang'e-6 missions, providing new insights into the evolution of organic matter in the solar system.The

science.title: Nasa ready for Artemis II splashdown as astronauts prep for Pacific recovery tomorrow
science.description: Nasa also assured that favourable weather is forecast for wind and wave conditions, precipitation, and thunderstorms at the time of Artemis II splashdown.

science.title: Humans reached Australia 60,000 years ago, new DNA study reveals
science.description: Scientists have uncovered compelling evidence that humans reached New Guinea and Australia around 60,000 years ago—earlier than some recent theories suggested. By tracing maternal DNA lineages, the team discovered that these early travelers likely used at least two different migration routes through Southeast Asia. This points to sophisticated navigation and seafaring skills far earlier than once believed. The research helps clarify a long-standing mystery about how humans spread across the globe.
